purpose: Canonical physics-engine configurations per genre. Numbers are tuned for a 60fps Phaser 3 Arcade Physics game running at 1280x720, derived from iconic genre exemplars (Mario, Sonic, Celeste, Hades, Geometry Wars, etc.) and standard Phaser tutorial defaults. Values are pixel-space (px, px/sec, px/sec^2) — NOT meters. Use as canonical defaults in code-generation prompts; tweak per-game from these baselines, do not invent.

units.time: milliseconds (ms) for grace windows; seconds for lifetimes

engine_choice_rules: arcade — default for 99% of 2D genres. AABB collision, velocity-based, no rotation physics. Cheap, deterministic, ideal for code-gen.
matter — only when ragdoll bouncing, stacked physics objects, or angular momentum is essential to gameplay (e.g. Angry Birds-style siege). Heavier, harder to tune deterministically.
none — turn-based grid games (strategy, board, sokoban, tetris). Use tweens for animation; collision is array/grid lookup, not a physics step.
three_3d — only for true 3D platformer/arpg. Three.js + Cannon-es or Rapier. NOT a fan-out target unless the prompt explicitly asks for 3D.

platformer.gravity_notes: 900 px/sec^2 is the Phaser tutorial sweet spot for snappy Mario/Celeste feel. Mario-style: 800. Celeste-style: 900-1000 (heavier fall for tight platforming). Sonic-style: 650 plus slope-based extra acceleration. Hollow Knight: ~700 with jump-cut. NEVER below 500 (floats), NEVER above 1500 (unplayable).

player_movement.notes: variable_jump_cut_ratio 0.5 = release-jump-early multiplies upward velocity by 0.5 (Mario-style). coyote 100ms + jump_buffer 100ms is the Celeste-validated sweet spot.

projectile_physics.notes: Optional. Most platformers don't shoot. If the player throws bombs/javelins use parabolic with gravity 600.

collision_setup: this.physics.add.collider(player, ground) — solid ground tilemap collision
this.physics.add.overlap(player, coins, collectCoin) — collectibles never block
this.physics.add.overlap(player, enemies, takeDamage) — enemy contact kills/damages, not blocks
this.physics.add.collider(enemies, ground) — enemies stand on ground
Do NOT collide enemies with each other (they push each other off ledges and stack badly)
platformer.world_bounds: scrolling — world larger than viewport; setBounds to level dimensions; cameras.main.setBounds same
platformer.animation_pattern: frame-based atlas with idle/run/jump/fall states; auto-flipX based on velocity.x sign
platformer.iconic_physics_signature: Variable jump height — release jump cuts upward velocity by ~50% (Mario), giving the player precise apex control. Without this, jumps feel binary and stiff.
common_physics_bugs: jump_cut_not_applied — player flies to full apex regardless of release; missing the 'on key release, if velocity.y < 0 then velocity.y *= 0.5' branch in update()
wall_jump_off_thin_air — checking onWall() via hitbox overlap instead of body.blocked.left/right; player can wall-jump while merely near a wall
1px_floor_seam_fall_through — adjacent ground tiles with non-collidable bottom edges; fix by using tile-index colliders not per-tile collisions
infinite_air_jumps — coyote_time not zeroed on jump; player keeps jumping mid-air
wall_stuck_at_max_speed — drag_x is 0 and player slams into wall; arcade physics zeros velocity but tunneling can occur at >1000 px/sec into thin walls

exemplars: Zelda: A Link to the Past
topdown.gravity_notes: Always 0. Top-down means no Y-axis falling. Set physics.config.arcade.gravity={x:0,y:0} explicitly.

player_movement.notes: Stop-start movement: zero velocity each frame, set from input. Phaser 3 tutorial standard is 250 for Zelda-feel. Hotline Miami is faster (~280) — speed > defense is its whole identity. CRITICAL: normalize diagonal velocity (multiply by 0.7071 = 1/sqrt(2)) or use Phaser's setVelocity().normalize().scale(speed) — otherwise diagonals are 1.41x faster.

projectile_physics.notes: Hotline Miami bullet speed is intentionally fast — being seen by a gun-enemy is near-instant death.

collision_setup: this.physics.add.collider(player, walls)
this.physics.add.collider(enemies, walls)
this.physics.add.overlap(player, enemies, takeDamage) — Hotline Miami: damage = death
this.physics.add.overlap(playerBullets, enemies, killEnemy)
this.physics.add.overlap(enemyBullets, player, takeDamage)
Do NOT collide enemies with each other
topdown.world_bounds: tile-based world, world larger than viewport; setBounds to map size
topdown.animation_pattern: 4-direction frame-based atlas: walk-up, walk-down, walk-left, walk-right; idle = first frame of each direction
topdown.iconic_physics_signature: Hotline Miami: instant death on contact — no health bar, speed > defense, retry is <1 second. Zelda: 8-axis movement with sword-swing locks rotation for 200ms.
common_physics_bugs: diagonal_speed_141x — forgetting to normalize diagonal vector; player visibly faster on diagonals
stuck_on_wall_corner — AABB sliding along corners; fix by setting body.checkCollision per-side or using slightly inset hitboxes
enemy_stacks_on_player — overlap() but no separation; for damage-on-touch this is correct, but if enemy is 'pushable' you need collider not overlap
facing_doesnt_update_on_idle — sprite_facing tied to velocity, so when velocity=0 the last facing freezes (correct) but devs forget to expose facing for shooting direction

arpg.gravity_notes: 0. Isometric/top-down. Z-axis is fake via sprite y-sort, not physics gravity.

player_movement.notes: Hades dash: ~700ms total animation but i-frames cover only the first half (~100ms). Dash is the SINGLE MOST IMPORTANT mechanic — Supergiant says every attack was designed around it. Dash distance ~140px (700 px/sec * 0.2s).

projectile_physics.notes: Some weapons fire homing projectiles (track player or nearest enemy), some pierce. Boomerangs (Diablo's Wirt's Leg / Hades's Aspect of Beowulf) use lifetime + reverse-velocity at 50%.

collision_setup: this.physics.add.collider(player, walls)
this.physics.add.collider(enemies, walls)
this.physics.add.overlap(player, enemies, takeDamage) — but NOT during dash i-frames
this.physics.add.overlap(playerBullets, enemies, damageEnemy) — pierce-aware
this.physics.add.overlap(enemyBullets, player, takeDamage)
Do NOT collide enemies with each other (clogs doorways)
arpg.world_bounds: room-based — each room is one screen; transitions reset camera bounds. Hades-style.
arpg.animation_pattern: 8-direction frame-based atlas; attack/dash/cast as separate states; sprite_facing via velocity OR mouse-cursor angle
arpg.iconic_physics_signature: Hades dash with i-frames — short distance (~140px), short duration (~200ms), invincible during animation. Combat is dance: attack-dash-attack.
common_physics_bugs: click_to_move_doesnt_update_target — player walks past click point because target is set on click but never updated as player moves
pathfinding_into_walls — cheap pathfinding picks a target inside a wall; fix by raycasting from player to target and clamping to last walkable tile
dash_iframes_persist_after_dash_ends — i-frame timer not cleared in update(); player invincible forever
dash_through_walls — dash sets velocity to 700 but no collision check during dash; player phases through walls. Fix: dash still respects collider, just gives i-frames vs damage.
enemies_stack_on_player — overlap() without separation. For melee enemies, this is correct (they damage on touch). For ranged enemies, add collider with player to keep distance.

player_movement.notes: Decoupled aim is the GENRE. Left stick / WASD = move. Right stick / mouse = aim AND fire. Player ship rotates to aim direction; movement direction can be opposite (strafe-fire).

projectile_physics.notes: Geometry Wars: continuous stream of bullets, very high fire rate (10/sec), bullets visually huge but hitboxes accurate. Bullets despawn off-screen.

splitter.notes: Geometry Wars Pinwheels — split into smaller versions on death

collision_setup: this.physics.add.overlap(playerBullets, enemies, killEnemy)
this.physics.add.overlap(player, enemies, gameOver) — instant death is the genre
Walls: for arena-bounded games, use world bounds + bounce on enemies
Do NOT collide enemies with each other (Robotron-style flow)
arcade.world_bounds: single-screen arena — setBounds to viewport, camera does not scroll. Player is restricted to arena.
arcade.animation_pattern: tween-rotation — Geometry Wars uses vector graphics, rotating ship + scaling enemies via tween, NOT spritesheet animation. Particles for trails.
arcade.iconic_physics_signature: Geometry Wars: decoupled aim/move + bloom-particle visual chaos + arena bounce on player ship. Robotron: single-screen, NEVER scrolls, enemies surround player from all sides.
common_physics_bugs: bullet_overlap_dies_to_lifetime_only — bullets fired off-screen never destroyed because lifetime > arena traversal time; fix by checking world bounds and destroying
splitter_recursion_explosion — splitter spawns 3 splitters that spawn 3 each; cap at depth 2
rotation_snaps_per_frame — using setRotation() with raw input; smooth with lerp or angle interpolation
fire_rate_uneven — using time.now % rate instead of last_fire_ms timer; uneven gaps in bullet stream
enemy_spawns_on_player — spawn enemies at random points without min-distance check from player

shmup.gravity_notes: 0. Side-scrolling or vertical-scrolling shmup; world scrolls past static-Y player.

player_movement.notes: Player ship hitbox is TINY (1-4px) compared to sprite (~32px). This is the bullet-hell signature. Player movement bound to lower 60% of screen typically. Galaga: single-axis (X only). R-Type: 2-axis but fixed X-floor.

projectile_physics.notes: Player bullets fast (700+ px/sec). Enemy bullets slower (200-400 px/sec) so they're dodgeable. Bullet hell games push enemy bullets to 300 with hundreds on screen.

formation.notes: Galaga: enemies enter in choreographed loops, settle into V-formation, then peel off in waves

collision_setup: this.physics.add.overlap(playerBullets, enemies, killEnemy)
this.physics.add.overlap(enemyBullets, player, gameOver) — small player hitbox
this.physics.add.overlap(player, enemies, gameOver)
Auto-destroy any sprite past camera bounds for performance
shmup.world_bounds: scrolling — viewport is fixed, world scrolls past. Vertical (Galaga, 1942) or horizontal (R-Type, Gradius). Player constrained to viewport.
shmup.animation_pattern: frame-based atlas with tilt animation when moving (R-Type: ship banks left/right). Boss = multi-part rigged sprite.
shmup.iconic_physics_signature: Tiny player hitbox (1-4px) inside large sprite — the DEFINING bullet-hell mechanic. Galaga: enemies fly in choreographed loops before settling into formation, creating threat-cycles.
common_physics_bugs: hitbox_equals_sprite — player dies from any near-miss; MUST shrink hitbox via body.setSize(4,4)
bullet_count_explosion_50fps — 200+ bullets on screen tanks framerate; use object pooling (Phaser Group with createMultiple)
scroll_speed_drift — using scrollFactor on tilemap but enemies positioned in world coords drift; lock to scroll velocity
bullets_collide_with_each_other — accidentally colliding playerBullets group with itself; explicit overlap calls only
enemy_spawns_off_screen_top_visible — spawn at y=-50 not y=-200; enemy pops into view

flight.gravity_notes: 0. Flight games suspend disbelief about gravity — ship moves freely.

player_movement.notes: Star Fox signature: thrusters and retro-rockets temporarily speed up/slow down within a forced-scroll. Player has momentum (acceleration + drag) unlike top-down stop-start. Roll/barrel-roll bound to a key — temporary i-frames.

projectile_physics.notes: Star Fox lock-on missiles use homing with a charged-shot mechanic.

collision_setup: this.physics.add.overlap(playerBullets, enemies, killEnemy)
this.physics.add.overlap(enemyBullets, player, takeDamage) — flight games typically have shield/health, not 1-hit
this.physics.add.overlap(player, enemies, ramDamage)
this.physics.add.overlap(player, terrainObstacle, takeDamage) — wing-clip damage
flight.world_bounds: rail/auto-scroll — world scrolls past at fixed (or thrustable) speed. Player constrained to viewport rectangle.
flight.animation_pattern: frame-based atlas with bank-left/bank-right/level frames driven by velocity.x; engine-thrust particle effect
flight.iconic_physics_signature: Star Fox: thrust + retro within a forced-scroll. Player has agency over forward speed within a window, creating dodge-around-obstacle gameplay impossible in fixed-scroll shmups.
common_physics_bugs: thrust_breaks_world_scroll — increasing player x doesn't match world scroll; world scrolls past faster than player thrusts forward, creating drift
barrel_roll_iframes_persist — i-frame timer not cleared
enemy_passes_through_player_at_high_relative_velocity — tunneling at >1000 px/sec relative; enable continuous collision in body.tilesAndPhysics
bank_animation_doesnt_return_to_level — only setting frame on key-down, not on key-up; ship stuck banked

obby.gravity_notes: 1000 — obby physics emphasize precision over Mario-feel. Roblox default jump-power 50 = jumps to 7.35 studs in ~0.5s, mapped to pixels = stronger gravity than platformer.

player_movement.notes: Roblox stud = 1 pixel mapping in 2D-obby translation. Roblox default WalkSpeed 16 studs/sec ≈ 220 px/sec at 14px/stud. Coyote/buffer slightly tighter (80ms) than platformer because obby precision is the genre.

projectile_physics.notes: Most obbies don't have player projectiles. Hazards = lava, kill-bricks, spinning-blades.

kill_brick.notes: On overlap = respawn at last checkpoint

collision_setup: this.physics.add.collider(player, platforms)
this.physics.add.collider(player, movingPlatforms) — momentum transfer
this.physics.add.overlap(player, killBricks, respawnAtCheckpoint)
this.physics.add.overlap(player, checkpoint, setCheckpoint)
this.physics.add.overlap(player, finishLine, levelComplete)
obby.world_bounds: scrolling — long linear course; setBounds to course extent
obby.animation_pattern: frame-based atlas with idle/run/jump/fall; respawn-fade tween on checkpoint
obby.iconic_physics_signature: Checkpoint-based respawn loop — death is instant, retry is instant, course is long. Frustration tolerance built around <2-second restart.
common_physics_bugs: checkpoint_not_saved_on_overlap — overlap triggers every frame inside checkpoint, accidentally resetting checkpoint position; use 'visited' flag
respawn_in_kill_brick — checkpoint placed inside an active kill brick zone; respawn-loop death
moving_platform_carries_player_double_speed — adding platform velocity to player velocity instead of just position; fix by snapping player.body.velocity.x to platform.body.velocity.x while standing
fall_off_world_no_respawn — missing world-bounds-bottom kill check; player falls forever

exemplars: Breath of the Wild (as inspiration for top-down adventure)
adventure.gravity_notes: 800 if side-scrolling adventure (Hollow Knight). 0 if top-down adventure (Zelda).

player_movement.notes: Hollow Knight gives all platformer abilities + dash + wall-jump + double-jump. Adventure differs from pure platformer in having mid-game ability unlocks (Metroidvania).

projectile_physics.notes: Soul/spell-based projectiles in Hollow Knight; resource-limited firing.

collision_setup: this.physics.add.collider(player, ground)
this.physics.add.collider(enemies, ground)
this.physics.add.overlap(player, enemies, takeDamage) — but NOT during dash i-frames
this.physics.add.overlap(playerAttack, enemies, damageEnemy)
this.physics.add.overlap(player, room_transition, loadNextRoom)
adventure.world_bounds: interconnected rooms — large world, room-based camera bounds
adventure.iconic_physics_signature: Metroidvania ability progression — dash unlocks new traversal, double-jump opens previously inaccessible rooms. Physics constants don't change but the player's MOVESET grows.
common_physics_bugs: dash_during_wall_slide_breaks_state — entering dash from wall-slide doesn't clear wall-slide flag; player dashes while gravity is still capped
double_jump_resets_on_landing_too_aggressively — using onCollide with ground; ANY ground touch (including 1-frame on a passthrough platform) refreshes; fix with on-ground state machine
wall_jump_off_corner_pixel — wall-detect via body.blocked.left fires for 1 frame on corner; player gets free wall-jumps off floor edges

rpg.gravity_notes: 0. Overworld is top-down; battle scene is none-physics tween-based.

player_movement.notes: RPG overworld speed slower than action top-down (160 vs 220) — exploration pace. Chrono Trigger uses smooth movement; Pokemon uses 16px-grid snapping. Battle scene = 'none' physics, all tween animation.

projectile_physics.notes: Battle-scene only. Spell projectiles = homing tweens to target sprite, fixed lifetime.

walker.notes: Overworld: enemies are scripted NPCs OR random encounters (no physics body)

battle_actor.notes: Battle scene: actors don't 'move' in physics sense; tween to attack position, tween back

collision_setup: this.physics.add.collider(player, walls) — overworld
this.physics.add.overlap(player, encounterZone, startBattle) — random encounter trigger
this.physics.add.overlap(player, npc, startDialogue)
Battle scene: NO physics colliders — turn-based, tween-only
rpg.world_bounds: tile-based world larger than viewport; setBounds to map size; battle scene = static/no scroll
rpg.animation_pattern: 4-direction frame-based atlas (overworld) + battle-pose atlas (idle/attack/cast/hurt) for battle scene
rpg.iconic_physics_signature: Two-mode physics — overworld is real-time arcade physics, battle is turn-based zero-physics tween system. Chrono Trigger broke tradition with seamless transition (battle in same scene), but most RPGs scene-switch.
common_physics_bugs: encounter_zone_double_trigger — overlap fires every frame inside zone; use 'in_battle' flag to gate scene transition
battle_actor_tween_overlap — tweening 2 actors to same spot; check destination occupancy before tween start
overworld_speed_too_fast_for_tile_grid — moving 200 px/sec but tiles are 16px = 12 tiles/sec, encounter probability tuning breaks; slow to 160

player_movement.notes: Enter the Gungeon dodge roll: 700ms full animation, i-frames in first half (350ms / ~175ms i-frame). Movement faster than ARPG (240 vs 220) because death is faster too.

projectile_physics.notes: Gungeon variety: pierce 1-3, homing, parabolic (grenades), boomerang. Default = straight-line fast bullet.

collision_setup: this.physics.add.collider(player, walls)
this.physics.add.collider(enemies, walls)
this.physics.add.overlap(player, enemies, takeDamage) — gated by dash_iframes_active flag
this.physics.add.overlap(playerBullets, enemies, damageEnemy)
this.physics.add.overlap(enemyBullets, player, takeDamage) — gated by dash_iframes_active
this.physics.add.overlap(playerBullets, walls, destroyBullet) — bullets stop at walls
Do NOT collide enemies with each other
action.world_bounds: room-based — Gungeon-style dungeon room layout, room transitions reset bounds
action.animation_pattern: frame-based atlas with attack/dash/hurt states; sprite_facing via mouse-cursor angle (decoupled from movement)
action.iconic_physics_signature: Dodge-roll i-frames as the core defensive verb. Hotline Miami: instant death + instant restart. Gungeon: dodge-roll + bullet patterns trained around it.
common_physics_bugs: iframes_dont_gate_overlap_callback — overlap fires regardless of i-frame state; gate inside callback with 'if (player.iframes > 0) return;'
dash_through_wall_with_iframes — dash sets velocity high but skips collider; player phases through wall during dash. Keep collider always-on; only damage gating is i-frame-controlled.
shoot_direction_uses_movement_not_aim — fire vector calculated from velocity instead of aim angle; can't strafe-fire backward
bullet_lifetime_too_long_clutters_screen — bullets persist 5 seconds, hundreds onscreen, tank framerate; 1.5s is the sweet spot

puzzle.gravity_notes: 0. Puzzle 'gravity' (Tetris pieces falling, Bejeweled gems falling) is simulated via tween or grid-step, NOT physics gravity. Implementing as physics gravity creates non-deterministic landings.

player_movement.notes: Sokoban: player moves 1 cell per keypress, tween 100ms to new position. Tetris: piece falls 1 cell per N ms (level-dependent), tween smooth. Bejeweled: swap = 200ms tween of 2 cells. NEVER use physics velocity — breaks grid alignment.

enemy_physics.notes: No enemies in classic puzzle. Sokoban has obstacles (walls, boxes); Tetris has 'gravity' as the antagonist (pieces falling at increasing speed).

collision_setup: Grid-array lookup: grid[y][x] === 'wall' before allowing move
NO Phaser physics colliders — all collision is array-based
Match-3: scan grid for runs of 3+ same-color, mark for clear, tween clear, gravity-collapse remaining
puzzle.world_bounds: single-screen — entire puzzle visible; setBounds to viewport
puzzle.animation_pattern: tween-based — tween position for movement, tween scale for clears, tween alpha for fade-out. NO frame animation typically.
puzzle.iconic_physics_signature: Tetris: piece falls 1 cell per X ms (level-dependent), accelerates with level. Soft-drop = fast manual fall; hard-drop = instant snap. Lock-delay = 500ms grace before piece locks (grants slide-under).
common_physics_bugs: tween_overlap_chains — player presses move during tween; queueing system needed or input is dropped
grid_desync_from_render — physics body says cell (3,4) but rendered sprite shows (3,5) due to tween mid-flight; only commit grid-coord at tween-complete
match3_cascade_in_one_frame — checking for matches inside the same frame as the tween-complete; chains visually skip; sequence with promises or 200ms delay between cascade steps
tetris_lock_no_grace — piece locks instantly on touch; no slide-under play possible; need 500ms lock_delay timer that resets on rotation/move

boardgame.gravity_notes: 0. Pure UI / state machine. Tween for card flips, dice rolls, piece moves.

player_movement.notes: All movement is click-driven, tween-animated. No keyboard input typically. Piece tweens from origin to destination over 300-500ms.

enemy_physics.notes: AI opponents are turn-based logic, not physics bodies.

collision_setup: NO physics — pure pointer-input on Phaser GameObjects with setInteractive()
Validation is rules-based: 'is this hex adjacent to a city?' = graph lookup, not collision
Drag-and-drop pieces use Phaser input.setDraggable() with snap-to-cell on dragend
boardgame.world_bounds: single-screen — whole board visible. Maybe pan/zoom for large maps (Catan).
boardgame.animation_pattern: tween-only — card flip = scaleX 0 then swap texture then scaleX 1. Dice = sprite atlas with random frame. Piece move = tween x/y over 400ms with ease-in-out.
boardgame.iconic_physics_signature: Turn-based state machine — explicit currentPlayerIndex, phase ('rolling', 'building', 'trading'), action queue. NO real-time physics. End-of-turn triggers next-turn cascade.
common_physics_bugs: turn_cascade_recursion — endTurn() recursively calls endTurn() through all AI turns in one frame; freeze during AI thinking. FIX: chain via setTimeout or scene.time.delayedCall.
click_during_tween_breaks_state — piece tween in progress, click registers another move, two pieces moving simultaneously; gate input via input.enabled = false during tweens
drag_release_outside_valid_cell — piece drops in space; snap back to origin or to nearest valid cell, never leave floating
ai_thinking_blocks_main_thread — heavy AI evaluation in single frame; spread across requestAnimationFrame chunks or use Web Worker

strategy.gravity_notes: 0. Pure grid/hex state machine. All movement is tween between cells.

player_movement.notes: Unit moves N cells per turn (movement_points). Path-tween from start to destination, hop-by-hop, 250ms per cell. Camera pans to follow unit during move.

projectile_physics.notes: Attack 'projectile' is animated tween (arrow flies, then sprite damage flash). NOT physics. Fire-and-forget tween with onComplete callback to apply damage.

ai_unit.notes: Same as player units; just AI-controlled. Pathfinding via A* on grid.

collision_setup: NO Phaser physics — grid-array lookup
isWalkable(x,y) checks tile type + occupancy
Pathfinding: A* with movement_points budget; valid moves highlighted on tile-select
strategy.world_bounds: tile/hex world larger than viewport — pan via mouse-edge or arrow keys. Civilization: huge maps (hundreds of tiles). XCOM: room-sized (10-30 tiles).
strategy.animation_pattern: frame-based atlas for unit idle + attack + hurt + death; tween for movement between cells
strategy.iconic_physics_signature: Turn-based state machine with phase enum: 'player_action', 'enemy_action', 'turn_resolution'. Into the Breach: enemy actions PRE-TELEGRAPHED at start of player turn — strategy comes from seeing what's coming.
common_physics_bugs: turn_cascade_through_all_enemies_in_one_frame — for-loop through enemies calls .act() synchronously; entire enemy turn happens in 1 frame, no animation visible. FIX: enemy turns chained via promise/scene.time.delayedCall, ~400ms per enemy

tween_to_blocked_cell — A* path computed at click time; another unit moves into path; tween proceeds and units overlap. FIX: revalidate path mid-move, or block other units from entering claimed path tiles
fog_of_war_reveal_in_one_frame — toggling fog tiles all at once; jarring. FIX: cascade reveal in 50ms steps from unit position outward
infinite_undo_stack — every action pushed to undo stack with full state copy; memory blows up. Cap stack at 10-20 entries.
ai_evaluation_freezes_60fps — heavy minimax in update loop; chunk via requestAnimationFrame or move to Worker

simulation.gravity_notes: 0. Top-down sim. 'Physics' is per-tick simulation, not Phaser physics.

player_movement.notes: Stardew Valley player walks; SimCity has no avatar (god-view). Stardew default ≈ 200 px/sec, 16x16 tiles. SimCity = camera pan only.

projectile_physics.notes: Sims don't fire (Stardew weapons are short-range melee tween).

monster.notes: Stardew mine monsters use top-down pathing on grid

npc_resident.notes: Stardew NPCs walk pre-scripted paths. SimCity citizens use flow-field on roads.

static_building.notes: Buildings are grid-cell occupiers, not physics bodies. Simulation tick updates their state (production, decay).

collision_setup: this.physics.add.collider(player, solidObjects) — for top-down sims with avatar (Stardew)
this.physics.add.overlap(player, interactable, showPrompt)
Building placement: grid-array lookup, not physics overlap
SimCity: NO physics at all — pure tile-grid simulation
simulation.world_bounds: tile-based world MUCH larger than viewport — full farm/city map; setBounds to map size
simulation.animation_pattern: frame-based atlas for player + npcs (4-direction walk); buildings = static sprites; particle effects for smoke/fire/water
simulation.iconic_physics_signature: Tick-based simulation — Stardew is 60fps but game-time advances at 700ms per game-minute (7 minutes real = 1 game day). SimCity: simulation-tick separate from frame-tick; simulation runs at 1-10 ticks/sec depending on game speed setting.
common_physics_bugs: simulation_tick_tied_to_framerate — production/growth runs every frame instead of every N seconds; framerate-dependent economy. FIX: explicit tick interval (every 1000ms increments game-time).
all_npcs_update_offscreen_every_frame — 100 NPCs each running pathfinding at 60fps; 6000 path-calcs/sec. FIX: only update visible NPCs every frame; offscreen NPCs update every 4-10 frames.

save_during_tween_breaks_state — saving while NPC mid-tween captures interpolated position; on load, NPC offset from grid. FIX: only save at tile-aligned positions, or store grid_x and tween-progress separately.
building_placement_overlaps — checking center-pixel only; large buildings overlap edges. Check ALL grid cells the building footprint covers.

3d-platformer.gravity_notes: Cannon-es / Rapier 3D gravity in m/s^2 (NOT pixels). -30 is the 3D-platformer canonical 'feel-heavy' value (vs Earth -9.8); Mario 64 uses ~3x Earth gravity in scaled units. Rapier is 5-10x faster than Cannon-es in benchmarks; prefer Rapier for new projects.

player_movement.notes: Mario 64 jump variants: single jump, double jump (chain), triple jump (high), long jump (forward distance), wall jump, side flip. Each is a state in the move state machine. Camera-relative input (W moves forward in camera direction, not world Z+).

projectile_physics.notes: Banjo eggs: arc with gravity. Mario throws shells: short-range parabolic.

collision_setup: Rapier: rigidbody.dynamic for player; rigidbody.fixed for terrain; rigidbody.kinematicVelocity for enemies
Use capsule collider for player (avoids snagging on edges)
Use trimesh collider for static terrain (cheap, accurate)
Use ball collider for collectibles (overlap-only, sensor=true)
Sensor flag: collider.setSensor(true) for triggers (no physical collision, fires onIntersection event)
3d-platformer.world_bounds: open 3D world (Mario 64 hub levels) or sectioned 3D world (Banjo); use AABB bounds for fall-out kill plane
3d-platformer.animation_pattern: skeletal animation (glTF skinned mesh) — Three.js AnimationMixer crossfades between idle/run/jump/fall/land
3d-platformer.iconic_physics_signature: Mario 64 triple-jump chain — single→double→triple, each higher than the last, requires landing-window timing. Banjo: hover-pad mid-jump extends air time, swimming uses fully 3D direction.
common_physics_bugs: capsule_snags_on_edge — using box collider for player; player gets stuck on 1-unit ledges. FIX: capsule collider with rounded bottom.
kinematic_enemy_pushes_dynamic_player_through_floor — kinematic body with high velocity rams player through floor. FIX: clamp enemy velocity, or make enemies dynamic with high mass.
camera_clip_through_wall — camera lerps to behind-player but no raycast; camera sees inside wall. FIX: raycast from player to camera target, clamp distance to first hit.
3d_input_not_camera_relative — W always pushes Z+ in world; player moves wrong direction relative to view. FIX: rotate input vector by camera y-rotation before applying.
frame_dependent_physics — running rapier.step() in every render frame without fixed timestep; 144Hz monitor breaks physics. FIX: fixed timestep (60Hz) with accumulator pattern.

3d-arpg.gravity_notes: Lower gravity than 3d-platformer because there's no jumping — gravity only matters for projectile arcs and ragdolls.

player_movement.notes: Camera fixed isometric (45deg pitch, ortho or low-FOV perspective). Click-to-move uses raycast from cursor to ground plane to find target. WASD optional for modern feel.

projectile_physics.notes: Most spells are straight-line at character height. Arc spells (Bone Spear) use parabolic. Boomerangs (Whirlwind range build) use lifetime-based reverse.

collision_setup: Rapier: capsule colliders for all actors
Trimesh for static terrain
Sensor colliders for AOE/zone effects
Player vs walls: collider (block movement)
Player vs enemies: collider (push), but damage via separate overlap check
Spells vs enemies: sensor + intersection event
3d-arpg.world_bounds: room/level-based; large 3D world streamed in chunks
3d-arpg.animation_pattern: skeletal animation with 8-direction movement blends + attack/cast/death
3d-arpg.iconic_physics_signature: Click-to-move with continuous target update — cursor-position raycast each frame, player walks toward cursor. Hold-LMB = walk-toward-cursor. Skill bound to RMB or hotkey.
common_physics_bugs: click_target_doesnt_update_during_movement — click sets target once; player walks past it. FIX: while LMB held, raycast every frame and update target.
raycast_misses_ground_falls_through — clicking on far edge of map, raycast hits sky; target unset, player stops. FIX: raycast with infinite ray or large maxDistance.
isometric_camera_orthographic_breaks_perspective — using perspective camera at low FOV simulates ortho, but characters at edges are stretched. FIX: actually use OrthographicCamera.
kinematic_enemies_phase_through_each_other — no enemy-vs-enemy collision; enemies stack on player. FIX: enemy-vs-enemy collider with low mass (allows push, doesn't deadlock pathing).
spell_collider_too_thin_misses_enemies — laser sensor with 0.1m radius slips between enemy capsules; widen to 0.3-0.5m sensor radius.

code_generation_directives: DEFAULT to physics_engine='arcade' for all 2D genres unless explicitly puzzle/board/strategy.
ALWAYS use the gravity value listed for the genre. Don't make up new gravity values.
ALWAYS implement coyote_time_ms and jump_buffer_ms for ANY platformer/obby/3d-platformer. The Celeste-validated 100ms is the sweet spot.
ALWAYS implement diagonal velocity normalization for top-down/arpg/action/shmup. body.velocity.normalize().scale(speed) — diagonals must NOT be 1.41x faster.
ALWAYS shrink player hitbox via body.setSize() for shmup (1-4px) and bullet-hell. Sprite size != hitbox size.
NEVER use physics gravity for puzzle game piece-falling (Tetris/Bejeweled). Use scene.time.addEvent or tween — physics is non-deterministic.
NEVER collide enemies with each other in arpg/action/twin-stick — they clog doorways and stack on player.
ALWAYS gate i-frame damage inside the overlap callback, NOT by disabling the collider — gameplay benefits from i-frame visual feedback.
ALWAYS put a 'turn_in_progress' flag on turn-based games (boardgame/strategy/puzzle) — without it, end-turn cascades all enemy turns in one frame.
ALWAYS use object pooling (Phaser Group with createMultiple) for bullets, particles, enemies in shmup/twin-stick — creating/destroying GameObjects per-frame tanks framerate.
FOR 3D, prefer Rapier over Cannon-es (5-10x faster) and Three.js with capsule colliders for the player.
